summary:
Epistaxis may occur after dental implant placement due to anatomical proximity between the maxilla and nasal cavities, particularly when surgery involves the maxillary sinus or causes trauma to nearby blood vessels. This indirect link arises primarily through procedural complications rather than a direct causative relationship.  

(Note: The summary focuses only on clinically significant associations without overreaching beyond supported findings.)

The provided information indicates an indirect but plausible association between epistaxis and dental implant placement due to shared anatomy—specifically, the closeness of the maxilla to the nasal cavity—and possible vessel disruption during maxillary-based implant procedures. Surgical trauma affecting surrounding tissues increases the theoretical risk for postoperative epistaxis despite lacking strong direct evidence linking implants directly to nosebleeds.
